Anonymous.Definition: Anonymous means something is created without giving it a name. It is often used for temporary objects, types, or functions.

Concatenation.Definition: Concatenation combines two or more values into a single value. It is most commonly used to join strings together.

ConditionalMemberAccess.Definition: The {c:bold}conditional-member access{/c} operator {c:keyword}(x?.y){/c} only proceeds if the conditional access target is not {c:color-blue keyword}null{/c}. Otherwise, evaluation of the expression is stopped and {c:color-blue keyword}null{/c} is returned immediately.

GenericMethod.Definition: A method designed to work with multiple types and must be passed a {c:bold}generic type argument{/c} when invoked. The generic type argument passed in will replace the {c:bold}generic type parameter{/c} defined in the method signature.

GenericType.Definition: A type that must be supplied a {c:bold}generic type argument{/c} when used. The generic type argument passed in will replace the {c:bold}generic type parameter{/c} in the type definition.

ImplicitCreation.Definition: The type does not need to be specified in this syntax because it is already defined earlier in the statement.

ImplicitlyTyped.Definition: The type is determined automatically by the compiler. The type is still fixed, even though it is not written explicitly.

ImportedNamespace.Definition: This {c:bold}using directive{/c} segment imports a {c:color-blue keyword}namespace{/c} defined in another file. Namespaces are imported to allow their types to be referenced {c:bold}without fully qualified names{/c}.

InterpolatedValue.Definition: An interpolated value is an expression embedded inside an interpolated string. Its result is inserted into the string at runtime.

Nullable.Definition: Nullable indicates that a value can also be {c:color-blue keyword}null{/c}. It is used to represent the absence of a value safely.

ReturnValue.Definition: The result produced by a method or function that is sent back to the caller when execution finishes.

ShortCircuit.Definition: When the left-hand expression is enough to determine the result, skip evaluation of the right-hand expression.

TypeParameter.Definition: A type parameter is a {c:bold}placeholder{/c} that is replaced by a concrete type when a generic type or method is used.

UsingStatementResource.Definition: The C# compiler will automatically call {c:color-yellow code}Dispose{/c}{c:code}(){/c} on this resource at the end of statement.
